seoTitle: How to Move a Jira Ticket Into a Sprint
metaDescription: Learn three ways to move Jira tickets into a sprint — drag and drop, right-click batch moves, and editing the sprint field directly.
intro: Leaving tasks stranded in the backlog makes it hard to see what your team is actually committed to this cycle. This guide covers the different ways to move a ticket into a sprint in Jira, from simple drag and drop to batch moving multiple issues at once.

steps.heading: Open the backlog
steps.body: Navigate to your software board's left sidebar and click the Backlog tab to see unassigned tasks sitting in the general work directory.

steps.heading: Drag and drop a ticket into a sprint
steps.body: Click and hold a ticket, then drag it up and drop it into the active or upcoming sprint container you want it assigned to.

steps.heading: Move multiple tickets at once
steps.body: Select several cards using shift-select or right-click, then choose the target sprint from the context menu to move them all together.

steps.heading: Move a ticket from the issue view
steps.body: Open a specific task, find the Sprint field, and choose the target iteration to assign it directly from the ticket.

steps.heading: Check sprint capacity
steps.body: Review the sprint footer metrics after moving tickets to make sure the new story point total still fits your team's capacity.

faq.question: How do I move a Jira ticket into a sprint?
faq.answer: You can drag and drop it from the backlog into the sprint container, use a right-click context menu for multiple tickets, or edit the Sprint field on the ticket directly.

faq.question: Can I move multiple tickets into a sprint at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, select multiple cards with shift-select or right-click and choose your target sprint from the context menu.

faq.question: How do I check if a sprint has room for more tickets?
faq.answer: Check the sprint footer metrics, which show story point totals so you can verify capacity before adding more work.

faq.question: Can I move a ticket into a future sprint that hasn't started yet?
faq.answer: Yes, both drag-and-drop and the Sprint field let you assign tickets to any active or future sprint that's already been created.
